text:
Yushohi (ウショウヒ, Ushouhi) Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 381 is a high-ranking soldier in the Royal Army of the Kakin Empire, and one of the personal soldiers of 1 st Prince Benjamin. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 34, Chapter 358 Yushohi was originally ordered by Benjamin to guard and spy on 11 th Prince Fugetsu's bodyguard, but was reassigned to join 8 th Prince Salé-salé's guard to assassinate him. 

Appearance

Yushohi has fair hair styled in a buzz-cut, sharp features, and a gaunt face. He wears the standard Kakin military attire of zipper jacket, pants, and dark combat boots. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 363 Like the rest of Benjamin's personal soldiers, he wears an earpiece in his left ear. 

Personality

Yushohi appears to enjoy killing people. While thinking about how his Stand by Me might be identified, he described his enjoyment in taking his time, but also said that he enjoys the moment a target of his dies even more. Despite this enjoyment, he maintains professionalism and remembers to do his duties, and does not display any outward emotion. 

Background

As one of the First Prince's soldiers, Yushohi graduated from Kakin's Royal Military Academy and became a part of his army. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 373 

Plot

Succession Contest arc

Right before the Black Whale sets off, Yushohi and Benjamin's thirteen other personal soldiers listen to their captain Balsamilco's briefing on the structure of the Black Whale 1, arrangements made for the safety of the Princes, and orders on how to act. 

After Benjamin returns to Room 1001 after the departure ceremony and speaks with Balsamilco, he orders his fourteen soldiers to both protect and report on all the other princes. Benjamin informs them to exercise extreme caution in ascertaining the unknown abilities of the Princes' Guardian Spirit Beasts and the Hunters' abilities, and authorizes them to use lethal force in self-defense. Yushohi becomes the Royal Bodyguard for Prince Fugetsu. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 36, Chapter 374 To avoid being detected by the En fields from nearby residential quarters, he chooses not to deploy his own. He attaches a Stinger Ball to Fugetsu, but before it can take effect, she teleports more than 20 meters away from him, terminating the ability. Given the proximity of Fugetsu and Kacho's rooms, it is likely that the time Fugetsu moved far enough to unknowingly terminate Yushohi's Stand By Me was when she used her Guardian Spirit Beast's ability to travel to Tier 3 on Day 4. This would imply that he first attempted to use his Stand By Me sometime that day. He uses other Stinger Balls as a simple way of identifying other Nen users hiding their identity in her service. 

On the fifth day, after Rihan's Predator counters Prince Salé-salé's Guardian Spirit Beast, Rihan requests to be substituted by Yushohi so the guardian-less prince can be assassinated with Stand by Me. Over Benjamin's personal soldier's communication system, Yushohi reports that Fugetsu's Guardian Spirit Beast is hypothesized to be able of translocation, but adds that it may be possible Fugetsu can become invisible or otherwise undetectable. Shortly after, Yushohi receives orders to change shifts with Rihan, and begins guarding Salé-salé. 

The night of the following day, Speculative but likely time. It is known that Yushohi's ability takes longer to activate on non-Nen users, and there are no signs that Salé-salé was a Nen user. Possible timing ranges from anytime on the fifth day to before dawn on the seventh day. Yushohi discreetly assassinates Prince Salé-salé and reports the matter to his superiors. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 37, Chapter 382 

Abilities & Powers

Yushohi has received military training. He has proven to be fairly analytical. 

Nen

Yushohi is a Nen user; however, his superior Balsamilco Might believes him to be less knowledgeable than Hunters in that regard. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 35, Chapter 364 He can use at least Hatsu and En, and therefore also Ren and Ten. Hunter × Hunter - Volume 11, Chapter 94 His Nen ability was extolled as the most suitable for assassination among Benjamin's 14 personal soldiers. 

Manga Appearances

Trivia

Yushohi's Nen ability (, satsujin wo muzai ni suru houhou; lit. the murderer's way of being innocent) is the Japanese name for ABC's television series How to Get Away with Murder.
The tankōbon release of Chapter 381 in Volume 37 added the name Stand by Me (スタンドバイミー, Sutando bai Mī) to Yushohi's Nen ability. However, this change was not present in the English volume.
Yushohi's hair is dark in a panel in Chapter 374.
